methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
SwarmFarm Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised AU$30M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 peers—1 public company and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

The peer-median method anchors the analysis. Across six same-sector, same-stage comparables, the median valuation is $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity within Series B robotics: 1X Technologies commands $10.0B while Fairmat trades at $350M. The median of $1.1B sits between these extremes and is less distorted by 1X's outlier valuation than a mean would be.

Given that only one valuation method is currently applicable, the triangulated range is $914M to $1.2B, centered on $1.1B. This suggests SwarmFarm's fair value likely falls in the $900M–$1.2B band. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peers, and commercial traction in agricultural automation—dimensions not resolved by peer multiples alone. Additional methods (venture capital method, revenue-multiple analysis) would strengthen conviction but require financial and forecast data not currently available.
